Private Endpoint always deployed in customer VNet by definition; VNet integration inherent to PE architecture; microsoft_managed | MCSB v3 baseline confirmed: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/azure-private-link-security-baseline

The assessed service IS Azure Private Link; Private Link support is meta-tautological for this resource type; microsoft_managed | MCSB v3 baseline confirmed: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/azure-private-link-security-baseline

PE has no public endpoint concept; all traffic via private IP in customer VNet; microsoft_managed | MCSB v3 baseline confirmed: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/azure-private-link-security-baseline

PE is network relay (NIC); stores no customer data; data classification applies to connected PaaS service (e.g. Storage SQL); not_applicable at PE resource level | Exa 2026-06-19: Private Endpoint is a NIC with a private IP address — it does not store any customer data. Data discovery and classification (Azure Purview, Microsoft Information Protection) applies to services with data stores (Storage, SQL DB, Cosmos DB). The PE resource itself has no data to classify. Apply DP-1 to the connected PaaS service. not_applicable at PE resource level. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/private-link/private-endpoint-overview

PE stores no persistent customer data; DLP targets services with data stores; not_applicable at PE resource level | Exa 2026-06-19: PE stores no persistent customer data. DLP solutions (Defender for Cloud DLP policies, Microsoft Purview DLP) target services with data stores. PE is a network relay — it routes connections but holds no data. Qwen3 reclassification marked this customer/True but final_verdict=not_applicable is correct. not_applicable at PE resource level. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/private-link/private-endpoint-overview

PE does not terminate or encrypt traffic; encryption handled by connected service (e.g. HTTPS to Storage TLS to SQL); not_applicable at PE resource level | Exa 2026-06-19: PE does not terminate TLS or perform any encryption. It forwards TCP connections from the private IP in the VNet to the connected PaaS service. Encryption is handled by the application layer protocol (HTTPS, TLS) between the client and the PaaS service backend — not by the PE NIC itself. Qwen3 reclassified as customer/True but final_verdict=not_applicable is correct. Apply DP-3 to the connected service. not_applicable at PE resource level. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/private-link/private-endpoint-overview

feature_name: Data at Rest Encryption Using Platform Keys
notes: PE stores no customer data at rest; platform key encryption not applicable at PE resource level | Exa 2026-06-19: PE is a NIC resource (ARM object). It has no data at rest beyond its ARM configuration (target resource ID, subnet reference, private DNS zone group, connection approval state). ARM configuration objects are protected by Microsoft-managed encryption at the platform level — not a customer-facing control. not_applicable at PE resource level. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/private-link/private-endpoint-overview

feature_name: Data at Rest Encryption Using CMK
notes: PE stores no customer data at rest; CMK not applicable at PE resource level; apply CMK to connected PaaS service if required | Exa 2026-06-19: CMK encryption requires customer data at rest in the service. PE stores no customer data at rest (only ARM config metadata). CMK not applicable. Apply CMK to the connected PaaS service (e.g., Storage CMK, SQL DB CMK) if required. not_applicable at PE resource level. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/private-link/private-endpoint-overview

feature_name: Key Management in Azure Key Vault
notes: PE has no customer-managed encryption keys; KV key management not applicable at PE resource level | Exa 2026-06-19: PE has no customer-managed encryption keys to store in Key Vault. No CMK support exists for the PE NIC resource. DP-7 covers KV certificate integration for the connected service — not applicable to PE itself. not_applicable at PE resource level. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/private-link/private-endpoint-overview

feature_name: Certificate Management in Azure Key Vault
notes: PE does not manage TLS certificates; cert management applies to connected service; not_applicable at PE resource level | Exa 2026-06-19: PE does not manage TLS certificates. It is a TCP-level forwarder (NIC). TLS certificate management applies to the connected PaaS service (e.g., App Gateway TLS cert via KV, Storage custom domain cert via KV). not_applicable at PE resource level. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/private-link/private-endpoint-overview

feature_name: Azure AD Authentication Required for Data Plane Access
notes: PE has no data plane (NIC routing traffic to PaaS service); data plane auth belongs to connected service; not_applicable at PE resource level | Exa 2026-06-19: PE has no data plane. It is a NIC that routes TCP connections — it does not authenticate callers. Data plane authentication belongs to the connected PaaS service (e.g., Entra ID auth to Storage, SQL DB). Management plane access to the PE ARM resource uses standard Entra ID via ARM RBAC. not_applicable at PE resource level. feature_name: Local Authentication Methods for Data Plane Access
notes: No local auth concept on PE; not_applicable at PE resource level | Exa 2026-06-19: No local authentication concept on PE data plane (PE has no data plane). No local user accounts, API keys, or connection strings belong to the PE NIC resource itself. not_applicable at PE resource level. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/private-link/private-endpoint-overview

PE makes no outbound Azure service calls requiring MI; MI applies to workload consuming PE not to PE NIC; not_applicable at PE resource level | Exa 2026-06-19: PE makes no outbound Azure service calls that would require a Managed Identity. PE makes no outbound Azure service calls requiring SP; not_applicable at PE resource level | Exa 2026-06-19: PE makes no outbound Azure service calls requiring Service Principal identity. SP usage for automation (e.g., Terraform provisioning PE) is a management plane concern covered by ARM RBAC, not an IM-3 data plane identity assignment on the PE resource. not_applicable at PE resource level. PE has no Entra ID-authenticated data plane; CA targets authenticated data planes; applies to connected service not PE NIC; not_applicable at PE resource level | Exa 2026-06-19: Conditional Access policies target authenticated Entra ID data planes (requiring Entra ID token). PE has no data plane — it routes TCP connections without authenticating callers. CA applies to the connected PaaS service data plane, not to the PE NIC. not_applicable at PE resource level. No Microsoft Defender plan for Private Link as a service; Defender covers connected PaaS services; feature_supported=Not Applicable in MCSB v3 baseline | Exa 2026-06-19: No Microsoft Defender plan exists for Private Link/Private Endpoint as a service. PE resource emits NO resource logs (feature_supported=False in MCSB v3 baseline); DiagSettings on PE.id returns no categories; network traffic via NSG Flow Logs on PE subnet | Exa 2026-06-19: Private Endpoint resource emits NO resource logs. feature_supported=False in MCSB v3 baseline (this is a direct 'False' not 'Not Applicable'). No native backup; PE config (target resource ID subnet DNS group) is declarative and IaC-recoverable; not_applicable in baseline | Exa 2026-06-19: No native backup capability for PE. All configuration is declarative: target resource ID (e.g., Storage account ID), group ID (e.g., 'blob'), subnet reference, private DNS zone group name and zone ID. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/private-link/private-endpoint-overview
